sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: Entact Bio has reinforced its position in induced-proximity therapeutics by appointing John G. Houston, Ph.D., to its Board of Directors, leveraging his experience in advancing Arvinas’s Veppanu (vepdegestrant), the first FDA-approved PROTAC drug. This strategic appointment supports Entact’s development of ENTAC™ medicines, which utilize the same underlying induced-proximity mechanism as PROTAC protein degraders but aim to enhance beneficial protein function rather than degrade harmful ones. The Encompass™ platform, rooted in deubiquitylase (DUB) biochemistry, enables the creation of these protein-enhancing medications to treat loss-of-function diseases previously out of reach for conventional therapeutics. While broader industry discussions at BIO 2026 highlighted artificial intelligence integration and Washington policy strategies, the specific advancement of Entact’s program underscores the maturation of proximity-inducing modalities beyond degradation. Concurrent regulatory shifts, including FDA pilot programs for early-stage trials and scrutiny over compassionate use pathways for obesity therapeutics, frame the environment in which these novel mechanisms operate. This development marks a significant step in translating the scientific foundation of protein degraders into a new class of precision medicines focused on functional enhancement, addressing underserved patient populations through innovative small-molecule drug development strategies amidst a complex regulatory landscape.

points.text: Entact Bio — ENTAC™ — Loss-of-function disease — Board Appointment of John G. Houston, Ph.D.
Arvinas — Veppanu (vepdegestrant) — PROTAC — First FDA-approved PROTAC drug (contextual reference)
Entact Bio: Developing precision medicines to enhance beneficial protein function via Encompass™ platform.
Arvinas: Developer of first FDA-approved PROTAC drug Veppanu (vepdegestrant).
ENTAC™: Induced-proximity mechanism enhancing beneficial protein function; Encompass™ platform; DUB biochemistry.
